reason: Original migration 0003 used 'metadata' as the column name; the SQLAlchemy model uses 'extra_data'. The to_dict() serializer exposes it as 'metadata' for clients. Tracked: see if the model should be renamed to 'metadata' in a future cleanup; until then, the two columns are kept in sync manually.

Same root cause as issue_artifacts.extra_data — the migration 0003 created the column as 'metadata' but the model declares 'extra_data'. The IssueComment endpoint isn't currently read through the ORM (everything goes via raw SQL / repository helpers), so this has not produced a 500 in production. Needs a follow-up migration to add the column.
Companion to the missing_in_db entry above. Same root cause.

Model declares 4 review-related columns that no migration has ever created. The handoff API exposes them only via to_dict(), and the front-end has not been wired to read them. Tracking ticket needed before next migration that touches the handoffs flow.
